title: Nonlinear frequency shift and bistability of magnon-polarons
authors: Kevin Künstle, Matthias Wagner, Philipp Knaus, Yannik Kunz, Ephraim Spindler, Katharina Lasinger, Matthias R. Schweizer, Philipp Pirro, John F. Gregg, Mathias Weiler
abstract: We investigate the nonlinear dynamics of strongly coupled surface acoustic waves (SAWs) and spin waves (SWs) in a magnetoacoustic resonator based on a YIG/ZnO heterostructure by combining microwave reflection measurements with microfocused Brillouin light scattering spectroscopy. In the linear regime, the electrical response reveals clear hybridization between standing SAW cavity modes and finite-wave-vector SWs, resulting in pronounced avoided crossings. At elevated drive powers, the hybrid system exhibits a strongly field-dependent nonlinear response characterized by a positive frequency shift of the driven SW mode. Using the vector Hamiltonian formalism for nonlinear spin-wave dynamics, we show that this shift is dominated by a cross-shift term. In our resonator geometry, this contribution becomes significant because the standing SAW cavity mode simultaneously excites counterpropagating SWs with wave vectors $+k$ and $-k$. For suitable field detuning, the nonlinear shift drives the SW mode into resonance with the SAW excitation, leading to a strong enhancement of the magnon population, broadband nonlinear scattering, and bistable foldover behavior. Beyond the foldover threshold, both the magnon and phonon responses stabilize. These results establish SAW-driven $k \neq 0$ magnon-phonon hybrids as a promising platform for nonlinear magnetoacoustics and wave-based information processing.
summary: This paper demonstrates that in a SAW-driven resonator, the excitation of counterpropagating spin waves leads to a unique positive nonlinear frequency shift and bistable 'foldover' behavior. These findings provide a new mechanism for controlling magnon-phonon interactions and suggest potential for wave-based information processing and power-limiting devices.
main_problem: The study investigates the origin of nonlinear frequency shifts and the emergence of bistability in strongly coupled magnon-phonon hybrids (magnon-polarons).
main_result: The researchers discovered that the nonlinear frequency shift is positive and dominated by a cross-shift term arising from the simultaneous excitation of counterpropagating spin waves (+k and -k) by the SAW resonator.
method: The study combines microwave reflection measurements (VNA) with polarization-resolved micro-focused Brillouin light scattering (uBLS) and utilizes a vector Hamiltonian formalism for theoretical modeling.
model_or_system: A magnetoacoustic resonator consisting of a YIG/ZnO heterostructure in a backward-volume magnetic configuration, where surface acoustic waves (SAWs) are strongly coupled to finite-wave-vector spin waves (SWs).
key_observables: Electrical reflection (S11), frequency-resolved magnon and phonon spectra, and power-dependent redistribution of magnon populations.
important_parameters: Strong-coupling regime (g/2pi = 5.05 MHz), acoustic wavelength (2 um), and magnetic field detuning relative to the anticrossing point.
main_assumptions: The nonlinear dynamics are modeled using the vector Hamiltonian formalism for nonlinear spin-wave dynamics.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the device geometry and measurement setup; Fig 2 details the linear coupling and experimental parameters; Fig 3 presents nonlinear coefficients and calculated dispersion relations; Fig 4 and 5 illustrate the power-dependent nonlinear shifts, hysteretic power sweeps, and the resulting bistability/foldover behavior.
paper_structure: The paper begins with the experimental setup and linear regime characterization, moves to the observation of power-dependent frequency shifts and nonlinear scattering, provides a theoretical explanation via the vector Hamiltonian formalism, and concludes with the demonstration of bistability and potential applications.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic of the sample and measurement setup. (a) Heterostructure consisting of piezoelectric ZnO deposited on a YIG/GGG stack. (b) SAW resonator patterned on the ZnO layer; the device is identical to that studied in Ref. [13]. (c) Combined optical and electrical measurement setup. The sample is mounted in BV geometry inside an electromagnet, allowing simultaneous measurements of the electrical reflec- tion and the polarization-resolved BLS response of excited quasi-particles

FIG. 2. Power dependent VNA reflection |S11| measurements. (c,e,g) |S11| parameter colorcoded in dependence of Bext and fVNA for an applied VNA power of PVNA = −30 dBm, 3 dBm and 10 dBm respectively. Three high-Q SAW modes (horizontal light green features with reduced reflection) are visible and show a pronounced anticrossing with the BV-SW mode at PVNA = −30 dBm. With increasing PVNA, the hybridization branches broaden and move to lower Bext. In addition, the color- coded |S11| data develop step-like features that depend on both magnetic field and frequency. (a) Frequency line cuts at fixed BS1 = 32.7 mT (line colors correspond to the dashed lines in panels (c,e,g)). Compared to the...

FIG. 3. (a) Nonlinear self-shift Wkk,kk and cross-shift W−kk,−kk coefficient as a function of wave vector k. The purple dashed line corresponds to the k of the driving phonon, which in the linear regime resonantly couples to the magnon. (b,c) Calculated linear spin-wave dispersions for the BV-SW mode (blue). The purple circles mark the SAW-driven states. In (b), the drive is resonant with the BV-SW in the linear regime. With increasing power, the positive total nonlinear shift detunes the resonance condition, as indicated by the red arrows and dashed red lines. In (c), the drive is initially off-resonant (lower Bext) and is shifted into resonance by the positive total nonlinear frequency...

FIG. 4. Observation of nonlinear processes using µBLS spec- troscopy. (a,b) Normalized µBLS intensity IBLS/IBLS,max as a function of fBLS measured in magnon and phonon polariza- tion at PMW = 10 dBm for two magnetic fields. The black and red dashed lines mark fdrive and 2fdrive, respectively. In (a), the field is tuned to the central anticrossing in the linear regime, and a nonlinear magnon signal is observed at 2fdrive. In (b), the field is below the linear anticrossing field, result- ing in a broad magnon spectrum, while a phonon signal is only present at fdrive in both cases. FIG. 5. Bistability in the coupled magnon-phonon system revealed by hysteretic PMW sweeps in µBLS and SA measurements. (a-c) Normalized integrated µBLS intensities, R IBLS/ R IBLS,max, for the phonon mode at fdrive and the magnon modes at fdrive and 2fdrive, measured during upward and downward PMW sweeps at the indicated magnetic fields. (d-f) Corresponding spectrum-analyzer response, expressed as αup/down = dPSA/dPMW,up/down. At the anticrossing field, BAC = 34.57 mT, all signals evolve continuously and no pronounced hysteresis is observed [panels (a) and (d)]. Spintronics.note: The paper directly studies the physics of magnon-phonon polaritons and nonlinear interactions in a magnetoacoustic heterostructure, which is a central theme of the researcher's work on the optics-spintronics interface.
